summary.intent: Implement tasks #297, #298, #299 — the framework-agnostic core of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue type in the ars-ui Rust component library, delivered as one combined PR.
summary.outcome: All 8 components and the ColorValue foundation were implemented, spec-synced, and pushed as PR #698 (Closes #297/#298/#299). After fixing a coverage CI regression, 8 rounds of Codex review addressed 40+ findings with TDD. As of the transcript end, pass-8 code is pushed and CI-green; reply/resolve/re-trigger for the final 8 threads is the next pending step.

repo: `core_maths::CoreFloat` is the workspace's no_std float tool; calls must use the fully-qualified form `CoreFloat::powf(x, y)` matching ars-i18n convention. It notably lacks `min`/`max`, requiring custom `fmin`/`fmax` helpers.
`MessageFn<Args, Out>` has no blanket From impl — each distinct closure signature requires a hand-written `impl<F: Fn(A, B) -> String> From<F> for MessageFn<...>` in message_fn.rs.

`Bindable<T>::get()` returns the controlled prop (parent-supplied); `set()` updates only the internal pending value. A `pending()` accessor was added this session — required for correct display/ARIA during drag in controlled mode.

Drag pattern: adapter drives DragMove/DragEnd from its own pointer listeners; core machine only receives the events. `on_change_end` fires as a typed `Effect::ChangeEnd` on DragEnd. The spec described nonexistent `use_platform_effects()`/`track_pointer_drag()` APIs that don't exist in the codebase.

`on_props_changed` + `props_output_changed` + `SyncValue`/`SetProps` events are mandatory for controlled-prop updates to take effect after mount; without them, controlled value/disabled/readonly go stale.
`Part::Marker { value: f64 }` cannot use `#[derive(ComponentPart)]` since f64 is not Eq/Hash; requires manual impls using `to_bits()` for hashing and bitwise equality.
The `snapshot-count` xci-fast gate requires at least one snapshot per machine state variant — AngleSlider initially failed because only 2 of 3 states had snapshots.

CI coverage uses `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace` then merges wasm lcov; ars-components threshold is 90% line / 90% branch. Running `-p ars-components` alone reads ~1.1% lower than the workspace run due to cross-crate test coverage.

Hue endpoint design: slider/area/wheel store an unwrapped `slider_value`/`x_value`/`y_value`/`hue_value` as the ARIA/position source of truth; `ColorValue` (hue ∈ [0,360)) is derived from it. This lets the 360° endpoint be represented in ARIA without violating the ColorValue invariant.
Disabled drag thumbs must explicitly set `tabindex="-1"` and `aria-disabled="true"`; they don't inherit from the disabled guard automatically.
RTL support: horizontal slider/area x-position CSS must mirror `(100.0 - pct)` when `ctx.dir == TextDirection::Rtl`; arrow key reversal was already handled but the visual position was not.
DragEnd must always be allowed through the disabled/readonly guard so a mid-drag state change doesn't wedge the machine in `Dragging` permanently.
Blur must be allowed through guards that block other events (disabled, empty items) to prevent the machine from getting stuck in `Focused` state.

code.finding: ColorValue::new only debug_asserts finiteness; release builds can construct NaN/inf. Added coercion of non-finite components to 0.0 before wrap/clamp.

from_hex used trim_start_matches('#') stripping ALL leading #, accepting ##3366ff. Changed to strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex). Also: byte-slices &hex[0..2] without is_ascii() check panics on non-ASCII input like #ああ.
parse_rgb_args/parse_hsl_args/parse_hsb_args used str::parse::<f64>() which accepts NaN/inf. Added parse_finite_f64 helper returning None for non-finite values.
format_color_string Hsb branch emitted hsb() without alpha; commit_input reparsed it opaque, silently setting alpha=1.0 on translucent colors. Fixed to emit hsba() and parse it.

Added 5 new From impls for color-specific closure signatures: Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,f64,&Locale), Fn(&str,&str,&Locale) for slider valuetext, Fn(&str,&str,&str,&Locale) for area valuetext.

format_value showed percentages (50) for 0..=1 channels but commit_input parsed 50 and clamped to 1.0. Fixed: percentage channels divide input by 100. ARIA spinbutton min/max/valuenow also needed *100 scaling.
SetProps updated channel/color_format but left ctx.input_text in old representation. When not focused, input_text must be reformatted from current value with new channel/format.
FocusUp/Down used grid columns value directly; columns:0 made row navigation a no-op. Must clamp columns to max(1, columns) before row arithmetic.
Initial render: focused_index was None, root had no tabindex, all items had tabindex=-1 — keyboard entry unreachable. Added tabbable_index() returning selected-or-first item for idle state.
Alpha track gradient used CSS 'transparent' as first stop (= transparent black), causing non-black colors to fade through black. Fixed to use same H/S/L at alpha 0 as the first stop.

Wheel thumb set aria-labelledby pointing to a *-label id but ColorWheel has no Label part — dangling IDREF. Removed it (aria-label was already set). Color slider DOES have a Label part; its aria-labelledby is correct.
Pre-existing map_or_else(.., identity) lint surfaced by stable 1.96.0; workspace denies clippy::suspicious so this was a CI blocker unrelated to the color work. Fixed incidentally.

workflow: The waiting-for-codex-review skill automates the Codex loop: post @codex review, poll 👀/👍 reactions, address findings TDD-first, sync specs, run cargo xci-fast, push, reply+resolve all threads citing test names, re-trigger. Each round must complete all steps atomically.

Spec sync should be delegated to a persistent subagent (SendMessage to continue the same agent) with exact change descriptions; this keeps specs consistent without inflating main context. The agent can be reused across all rounds of review.

For multi-component implementations, read authoritative spec sections directly rather than relying on agent paraphrases — agent reports had multiple inaccuracies (wrong function arity, wrong module paths, wrong ColorValue location) that caused bugs.
Background watcher scripts in zsh: avoid `status` as a variable name (it is read-only in zsh). Use names like `cov_state` or `verdict` instead.

Verify CI coverage locally before pushing with: `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace --lcov > /tmp/lcov.info && cargo xtask coverage check-all` — this exactly replicates what CI measures.

When a Codex thread appears to be a stale re-raise (line: null anchor, same issue as a prior round), verify the fix is in HEAD, reply citing the specific test name, then resolve without re-implementing.

friction: Codex was rate-limited ('You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews') for the first two @codex review triggers, blocking the mandatory review gate entirely.

The spec had extensive drift from actual codebase APIs: nonexistent use_platform_effects()/track_pointer_drag(), wrong ColorValue module path (components/color_picker.rs vs ars-core/src/color.rs), wrong color_name_parts() arity, missing type Effect declarations — required systematic reconciliation across all 8 spec files before any commit.
8 rounds of Codex review (40+ findings), with later rounds increasingly focused on controlled-mode ARIA edge cases on agnostic core that has no adapter consumer yet.
zsh read-only `status` variable crashed a background CI-watcher script; had to rename to `cov_state`.
Tool safety classifier became temporarily unavailable mid-session, blocking the reply/resolve/re-trigger bash execution for several minutes.

Coverage CI failed after initial push (ars-components branch 89.7% < 90%); required both-arms attribute tests across 6 components AND discovery of a real dead-branch bug (has_description field had no setter event, making aria-describedby permanently unreachable).
core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max methods — not documented; required manually inspecting the trait. Required custom fmin/fmax helpers.
AngleSlider snapshot-count gate failure discovered only after the initial push: xci-fast requires ≥1 snapshot per state variant; needed to add Idle/Dragging/Focused root snapshots.
open_items: Pass-8 thread reply/resolve/re-trigger is the immediate next step — 8 threads need replies (M1-M8), then @codex review re-triggered and a new watcher started.
PR #698 awaits Codex 👍 before merge; issues #297/#298/#299 auto-close on merge via keywords in PR body.
Adapter specs (leptos/dioxus-components/specialized/color-*) describe future framework adapter code — explicitly out of scope for #297-299, to be a separate adapter task.

ColorValue spec currently lives in color-picker.md §Types; could be promoted to spec/shared/color-types.md to decouple it from the color-picker component (surfaced but not landed — touches manifest + 8 frontmatters).

spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md MessageFn listing omits the new color-component signatures (Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), new slider/area valuetext signatures) — left intentionally pending a decision on whether to list all component-specific impls or none.

The circular hue convention (360°≡0° in ColorValue, but displayed as 360° in slider/wheel ARIA via unwrapped tracking fields) is an implicit design decision that warrants a brief doc note in the spec or CLAUDE.md.
